name: Frankfurt am Main (Hesse, Germany)

colors.symbolism: the silver of the city's heraldic field
the city's historic livery and the eagle's tongue
the gold of the imperial eagle's crown

description: A horizontal bicolour of white over red, with the city's coat of arms (a crowned single-headed eagle) charged on the centre.
history: Frankfurt's white-and-red colours and the imperial eagle date from the city's status as a Free Imperial City of the Holy Roman Empire; coronations of Holy Roman Emperors took place in Frankfurt's cathedral from 1562 onwards.

features.symbolism: the historic civic colours of Frankfurt
recalls Frankfurt's status as a Free Imperial City and seat of the imperial coronation
